mission.title: the-update-framework-tuf-: Modernise and rationalise packaging
mission.description: Modernise and rationalise packaging. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current the-update-framework-tuf- deployment
steps.description:
Verify your the-update-framework-tuf- version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n the-update-framework-tuf- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=the-update-framework-tuf-
helm list -n the-update-framework-tuf- 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working the-update-framework-tuf- installation.

steps.title: Review the-update-framework-tuf- configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant the-update-framework-tuf- configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n the-update-framework-tuf- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=the-update-framework-tuf-
kubectl get configmap -n the-update-framework-tuf- -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=the-update-framework-tuf-
```
**Description of issue or feature request**:

Python packaging has changed significantly since the tuf packaging was created.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Modernise and rationalise packaging
steps.description:
This is a WIP  trying to identify whether splitting the client and repository code and maintaining a client as
a separate installation (in a client branch for now) will be useful for client side users of tuf (e.g. pip) as well as for providing some kind of support for older versions.

Up to now I
```yaml
pip install -e ../vendoring
ERROR: File "setup.py" not found. Directory cannot be installed in editable mode: /home/jku/src/vendoring
(A "pyproject.toml" file was found, but editable mode currently requires a setup.py based build.)
```

steps.title: Upgrade the-update-framework-tuf- to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade the-update-framework-tuf-:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade the-update-framework-tuf- the-update-framework-tuf-/the-update-framework-tuf- --namespace the-update-framework-tuf-
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n the-update-framework-tuf-
helm list -n the-update-framework-tuf-
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n the-update-framework-tuf- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=the-update-framework-tuf-
kubectl get events -n the-update-framework-tuf- --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Modernise and rationalise packaging" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This is a WIP  trying to identify whether splitting the client and repository code and maintaining a client as
a separate installation (in a client branch for now) will be useful for client side users of tuf (e.g. pip) as well as for providing some kind of support for older versions.

Up to now I have only removed the pure repository code and generated a tuf-client distribution.
codeSnippets:
pip install -e ../vendoring
ERROR: File "setup.py" not found. Directory cannot be installed in editable mode: /home/jku/src/vendoring
(A "pyproject.toml" file was found, but editable mode currently requires a setup.py based build.)

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with the-update-framework-tuf- installed or the issue environment reproducible.
